title: Review the recent recovery-first policy changes in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator. Focus on whether the Claude-provided summary is accurate and whether any immediate-BLOCKED path still remains. Check AGENTS.md, .codex/prompts/kernel.md, .codex/prompts/k.md, rules/shared-orchestration-playboo...
summary_text: **Findings** 1. High: An immediate fail path still exists in policy text for hybrid failover. `AGENTS.md` says when Claude is degraded/exhausted, all tasks run codex-only and MCP-dependent tasks “will fail and require manual retry,” which bypasses recovery-first semantics. Refs: [AGENTS.md:47](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:47), [AGENTS.md:49](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:49), [AGENTS.md:121](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:121), [AGENTS.md:129](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:129) 2. High: `run-kernel-council-review.sh` still has a direct fail-closed path with no in-script recovery matrix loop (no re-probe/fallback/backfill attempt before exit). It builds/runs the lane matrix once, then exits on aggregate failure or non-pass gate. Refs: [run-kernel-council-review.sh:142](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/run-kernel-council-review.sh:142), [run-kernel-council-review.sh:246](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/run-kernel-council-review.sh:246), [run-kernel-council-review.sh:273](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/run-kernel-council-review.sh:273), [run-kernel-council-review.sh:326](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/run-kernel-council-review.sh:326) **Claude-summary accuracy** - Mostly accurate on docs/prompts/checks adding recovery-first language and assertions: [kernel.md:33](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:33), [k.md:36](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/k.md:36), [shared-orchestration-playbook.md:80](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/rules/shared-orchestration-playbook.md:80), [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:71](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:71), [check-codex-vote-prompt.sh:143](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh:143) - Not fully accurate if it claimed “no immediate-BLOCKED path remains,” due the two findings above. **Residual risk / test gap** - I could not complete full end-to-end execution of both check scripts in this sandbox because `mktemp` creation is denied here; static assertions ran and were passing up to that environment failure point.
